CONSULTANT AGREEMENT — template (a GovCon consultant providing federal-contract procurement services to a client company, on a profit-share basis)

Parties: a Consultant LLC and a Company (client), for the Consultant to provide professional Services and Deliverables described in Appendix A.

1. SCOPE OF WORK — Consultant provides the Services and Deliverables in Appendix A.
2. TERM/TERMINATION — in effect until the earliest of: completion of all services; 12 months from execution; or as otherwise provided. Renewable by mutual agreement (e.g. up to 5 years). Terminable by either party on 60 days' written notice; Consultant is paid for services performed prior to termination (including authorized work during the notice period). On completion/termination, Consultant returns all Company data, materials, and work product (other than the Consultant's own materials).
3. PAYMENT FOR SERVICES — Company pays per the Fees in Paragraph 9; Consultant submits an itemized monthly invoice for services and authorized expenses; invoices payable within 30 days.
4. INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR — Consultant performs as an independent contractor; no partnership, joint venture, agency, or employment relationship is created.
5. COMPLIANCE WITH LAWS — comply with all applicable federal/state/county/local laws.
6. PROPRIETARY INFORMATION — all non-public knowledge/information learned from the Company is the Company's confidential, proprietary information, protected per a separate Confidentiality, Non-Disclosure, Non-Circumvention Agreement.
7. REPRESENTATIONS & WARRANTIES — Services performed professionally by well-qualified individuals; Consultant provides qualifications on request.
8. WORK POLICY — observe the Company's working hours, rules, security, and holiday schedule when on premises; employ reasonable efforts to meet deadlines and documentation standards; meet regularly to review progress.
9. PROFIT SHARE — net profit accrues for the term (or as long as any contract executed during the term lasts) and is disbursed per the agreed proportions (example: Company 75% / Consultant 25%), determined upon contract execution / notice of award. The Company provides the Consultant a subcontract agreement equal to the profit-share dollar equivalent on earned contracts before work commences.
10. DISTRIBUTION OF PROCEEDS — on payment for work, the Company is reimbursed 100% of its capital contribution before profit shares are calculated; net profits are then distributed per Paragraph 9 after payroll, subcontractor, material, employee, and reasonable attorney costs.
11. GENERAL PROVISIONS — headings for convenience; waiver; severability; modification only in writing signed by both parties; governing law (e.g. State of Florida, with jurisdiction/venue there); entire agreement (this Agreement plus Appendix A).

APPENDIX A — SCOPE:
Consultant assists the Company in procuring U.S. federal contracts: requirements/specifications analysis, marketing, research, and preparation of bids, proposals, and negotiations. Deliverables include client research, marketing, findings and recommendations in a uniform format, and preparing/assisting with technical proposals for Sources Sought, RFPs, and similar documents.

Owner (Company) expectations: attend all required meetings with government officials; attend or send a representative to all events tied to a specific contract; agree to bid at least 75% of projects deemed critical to revenue goals; provide all information needed to facilitate the bid package on time; send a representative to any site visit; make recommended changes to government marketing materials (SAM profile, capability statement, website).

Consultant duties: identify a Top-10 target list based on client preferences (geography, project type, size); market the company to agencies for sole-source and set-aside projects; develop relationships with agency officials for non-publicized opportunities; provide RFP submittal documents/attachments/supplemental materials and help assemble missing sections (the owner assembles the final package); review the final RFP for thoroughness and completeness; source RFPs and set up interviews to staff the project.
